english: bcóm-mo (bcom mo) an exclamation like: I am done for! perii! - 2. to destroy, towns etc. Glr.; bcóm-la bźág-go (bcom la bzhag go) id. Glr. - 3. to plunder, spoil, rob, jóms-pai grabs byás-pa-la ('joms pa'i grabs byas pa la) as they were about to rob him Mil. - 4. to finish, accomplish W., cf. ćóm-pa (com pa).

1. C., also rźor (rzhor), hoe, grubbing-hoe, mattock, pick-axe (W. *tóy-tse*), jór-gyis rkó-ba ('jor gyis rko ba) to turn up with the hoe; jór-po ('jor po) a large mattock, pick-axe, spade, jór-bu ('jor bu) a small one, a hoe; jor-yú ('jor yu) the handle of a hoe, jor-lcágs ('jor lcags) the iron of a mattock Cs. - 2. supine of jó-ba ('jo ba).

I. vb. 1. to hang down, of a cow's udder, of the long hair on a yak's belly, of tails etc.; jòl-jòl ('jol 'jol) hanging-belly, paunch. - 2. gen. _byól-ba ('byol ba) to turn aside, to make way. II. sbst., also (Cs.) jòl-jòl ('jol 'jol) and rźól-ba (rzhol ba), train, trail; retinue Cs.; jól-gos ('jol gos) Cs., jól-ber ('jol ber) Wdk., Pth., a robe or garment with a train; jól-can ('jol can) having a train; jól-méd ('jol med) without a train Cs.
hanging, cf. pýań-né (phyang ne), gród-pa jòl-lé (grod pa 'jol le) hanging-belly, paunch, cf. pyal (phyal) Lex.

acc. to the descriptions given by natives, a bird of the size of a blackbird, of lively motions and an agreeable whistling, in the neighbourhood of Lhasa, building in willow-trees and thorn-bushes; Cs. has: a turkey-hen.
W., service done in socage, compulsory service, in the fields, on roads etc.

also rje-u (rje'u), lord, master, 1. ruler, king, yúl-gyi rje mdzád-nas (yul gyi rje mdzad nas) ruling over a country, acting the part of a sovereign Glr.; bod-káms-kyi rjé-bor gyur (bod khams kyi rje bor gyur) he became sovereign of Tibet Wdk.; sá-yi bdág-po mi-yi rje (sa yi bdag po mi yi rje) Mil. lord of the ground, ruler of the people; rjé-bo dań bran (rje bo dang bran), rje-kól (rje khol) Stg., master and servant; rje-blón (rje blon) king and minister; rje ći lags (rje ci lags) sir, what does that mean? Glr.; also a title before names, esp. names of kings, jó-bo rje Dipangkára (jo bo rje Dipangkara) Glr.; rje-bdúd rje-btsán (rje bdud rje btsan) the gentlemen devils and the gentlemen goblins (messieurs les diables et messieurs les farfadets); rje dkon-mcóg-la gsól-ba _débs-pa (rje dkon mchog la gsol ba 'debs pa) Mil. is in fact an empty phrase in the mouth of a Buddhist philosopher, but may nevertheless be used in Christian language for addressing God as 'our Lord'. - 2. a nobleman, a person of rank, rjeu(i) rigs (rje'u'i rigs), rje-rigs = rgyal-rigs (rje rigs = rgyal rigs) the caste of nobility. - rje-dpón (rje dpon) (Lex. आर्य) = rje (rje), master, lord, prince Cs.; rjé-ma (rje ma), also rćés-ma (rces ma) Cs., col. *śé-ma*, a lady of rank, rje-cúń (rje chung) a young lady, a miss; rjé-srás (rje sras) a young gentleman; also a term of address Cs. - rje-btsún (rje btsun) reverend sir, a title of the higher priesthood, rje-btsún-ma (rje btsun ma) fem. - rjé-sa (rje sa) (or źé-sa (zhe sa)) byéd-pa (byed pa) to show deference, to pay one's respects; źé-sai gtam (zhe sa'i gtam), or źé-sai skad (zhe sa'i skad) courteous words, esp. ceremonial and complimentary terms, e.g. dbu (dbu) for mgo (mgo) etc. W.: *yá-śa ćò-će*, *yá-śę pé-ra*.
the lower part of the leg, the shank (W. *sug*); rkań-lág rje-ńár (rkang lag rje ngar) the lower part of the arms and the legs Med.

pf. brjes (brjes), fut. brje (brje), imp. brjes (brjes), W. *źé-će*, to barter, to give or take in exchange; _di-dag-gis brjeo ('di dag gis brje'o) it may be exchanged for these Dzl.; *zan dań srog źé-će* W. to risk one's life for the necessary food (as thieves do); brjé-byai nor (brje bya'i nor) articles of barter; in a more general sense: to change, to shift, miń (ming) the name, gos (gos) the clothes Dzl., gnas (gnas) the place, tse (tshe) the life; i.e. to die Cs. - brjé(-ba)-po (brje ba po) a barterer Cs.

pf. and fut. brjed (brjed) 1. to honour, reverence c. dat., mcód-ćiń brjéd-pa (mchod cing brjed pa) id. Dzl.; brjéd-pai _os (brjed pa'i 'os) venerable Lex. - 2. to forget, frq. (cf. lus-pa (lus pa)); brjéd-du _júg-pa (brjed du 'jug pa) to make forget, to cause to forget.
